<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Crushing Coils](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4906)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from constrictor snakeskin, the strips of this _[+1 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_ wrap around you like an [anaconda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3203) might wrap around its victim. The first time you roll a 1 on any attack roll or check after donning the armor, it fuses with you and constricts. It constricts anytime you roll a 1 on any attack roll or check thereafter. When the armor constricts, you’re [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) for 1 round.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** The armor wraps around you, allowing you to don it by the time the activation finishes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crushing Coils

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Draconic Toxin Bottle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4907)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glass bottle appears to be a [_dragon's breath potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=185) of a specific dragon type (usually [red](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168)) but has been polluted, making it toxic to use.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You drink from the bottle and must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude saving throw or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure) and immediately vomit the potion directly onto yourself, taking damage as though you had failed a save against a standard _dragon’s breath potion_. If you succeed at your Fortitude save, you become sickened 1 but otherwise are able to unleash a breath weapon as normal for a _dragon’s breath potion_ of the appropriate type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Draconic Toxin Bottle

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 191 **Bulk** L --- The origins of the notorious _Dullahan Codex_ are shrouded in mystery. Some legends claim it belongs to a [dullahan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2964) whose head was taken by the [Grim Reaper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3036). Others attribute its creation to a powerful [necromancer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3538) whose name has been lost to time. Whatever the truth, the grimoire has passed down through the ages, sometimes via mortal hands and other times mysteriously appearing among the possessions of its next target. It deserves its reputation for dooming those who possess it to die, but scholars debate whether the codex causes this fate or merely acts as its harbinger. The _Dullahan Codex_ is a jet-black tome bearing a single rune embossed on its cover, and it functions as a _[true endless grimoire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=992)_. Inside, scrawled across its parchment pages in a delicate, spidery script, is a lengthy list of names that always appears in a reader’s native alphabet. The grimoire isn’t sentient, but it selects its owners, quickly passing out of the hands of those it doesn’t choose. An intended victim’s name appears on the list of names. If your name is on the list and you touch the volume or read from its pages, you must attempt a DC 45 Will save.
**Critical Success** The codex disappears, moving on to a new victim.
**Success** The codex fuses to you.
**Failure** The codex fuses to you, and you become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but you’re doomed 2. If you attempt to get rid of the codex while it’s fused to you, it returns to your possession within an hour. Each day the codex is fused to you, you must attempt another Will saving throw, but a critical success does nothing. The doomed value from the codex can decrease only after it’s no longer fused to you; once it’s fused to you, you remain its intended victim unless you complete a [_freedom_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=193) ritual aimed at ending this 10th-level effect. (On a critical failure with this ritual, the codex adds all casters to its list.) You can redirect the curse by inscribing another person’s name in the grimoire and succeeding at a DC 40 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check. Doing so is an [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) act. If the curse is ritually ended or redirected, or the chosen victim dies, the codex moves on to a new victim.
name: Dullahan Codex

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Gaffe Glasses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4909)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These wire-rim glasses appear to be [_glasses of sociability_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1057), making you think they grant a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) when they grant none. The GM secretly adjusts your Diplomacy checks to ignore the bonus, and if you have multiple +1 item bonuses to Diplomacy, the glasses take precedence, negating those bonuses.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** Like _glasses of sociability_, with the same limitations, you stare at another creature. If you’ve met and exchanged names, you expect to instantly remember the target’s name. However, you recall the worst possible incorrect name, such as mistaking a famous artist for their hated rival. This blunder doesn’t prevent you from realizing the creature’s real name after you’ve been corrected. Once you use this activation, the glasses fuse to you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gaffe Glasses

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Hat of Disagreeable Disguise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4910)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 191

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant hat appears to be and functions as a _[hat of disguise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=442)_. When you invest the hat, it fuses to you.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Like a _hat of disguise_, the hat casts a 1st-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) spell on you. However, the illusion disadvantages you based on your intent, making you, for example, appear to be a suspicious ruffian if you’re trying to sneak past guards or lending you the seeming of a pauper if you’re trying to impress a shallow aristocrat. You and those you consider to be allies must succeed at a DC 16 Will save or you perceive the illusion as you intended it, though others won’t. Evidence to the contrary allows you to attempt to disbelieve the false version of the illusion. You can’t [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Hat of Disagreeable Disguise
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Hat of Disagreeable Disguise (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You can Activate the hat as a 2-action activity any number of times per day, the _illusory disguise_ is 2nd level (allowing it to malfunction with sound and smell), and the DC is 23.
name: Hat of Disagreeable Disguise

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Heckling Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4911)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk**  as tools of the normal type
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +9; precise vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [_telepathy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719) (two common languages)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, one skill associated with their use +9

**Int** +2, **Wis** +2, **Cha** +2

**Will** +7 

</column>

---        

Tools that are severely misused or left in malevolent circumstances can develop malicious sapience, dedicated to critiquing those who use them. Such _heckling tools_ are often born from implements useful to adventurers because such people are the likely to misuse tools or leave them in a corrupting situation. When you first set to using the tools, they fuse to you. Used for their intended purpose, the tools telepathically badger and disparage you, mocking your abilities and giving you ill-founded advice. You must succeed at a DC 19 Will save to realize this badgering comes from the tools and not your own negative thoughts. Instead of the tool’s usual bonus, you take a –2 circumstance penalty to checks made using _heckling tools_. Once you realize the tools are cursed, you can suppress their negative effects, gaining their typical bonus for 24 hours if you succeed at a DC 17 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check to placate them, often by offering obsequious, public admiration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Heckling Tools

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Luckless Dice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4912)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=110" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved of bone, _luckless dice_ appear to be a set of [loaded dice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1333). If unsuccessfully identified as [cursed](/Traits.aspx?ID=567), _luckless dice_ seem to hold a minor [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) that improves your luck. However, they fuse to you when you use them, cursing you with ill fortune. _Luckless dice_ don’t grant a bonus on [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks. Instead, when you use them to gamble, the GM rolls secretly, granting you the lower result. Once you realize the dice are cursed, the GM can instead allow you to roll twice and take the lower result.

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Mother Maw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4913)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** placed on a surface

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This item appears to be and functions as a [_portable hole_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2195), but it’s actually the maw of an alien extradimensional creature akin to and older than a bag of devouring. Any animal or vegetable matter put in the hole has a chance of triggering the creature’s interest. Whenever you reach into the hole to retrieve an item or place an animal or plant product within the bag, roll a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, the hole ignores the intrusion. On a failure, the _mother maw_ devours the triggering material, removing it from existence. The maw can’t eat artifacts. If the triggering material isn’t entirely inside the maw, such as when someone reaches inside, the _mother maw_ attempts to pull it completely inside using a [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) action with a +28 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) bonus. On a success, it devours the victim or object.

Whenever the maw critically fails the Athletics check, it regurgitates one creature or object it previously devoured. The condition of the vomited creature or object depends on its resilience and the time it has spent inside the maw. Typically, the maw spews forth items it finds difficult to digest, such as those made of [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) or protected by magic. It can and does regurgitate remains, though.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mother Maw

[Shared-Pain Sankeit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4915)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 192

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=53)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _shared-pain sankeit_ seems to be an impressive suit of _[+2 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [fortification ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2789) darkwood [sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=53)_ shaped to resemble a mighty linnorm. However, it protects you by drawing on the health of your nearby allies. When you roll the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) for the armor’s fortification rune, it protects you normally when you roll a 20. When you roll a 17, 18, or 19, though, the GM determines damage for the critical hit normally, then distributes half to you and the other half evenly among allies within 30 feet of you. If no ally is within range to take the distributed damage, the _fortification_ rune fails to function. This effect manifests the first time an attacker scores a critical hit on you, after which the armor fuses with you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shared-Pain Sankeit

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A skull-topped metal shrieking key appears to be a [_skeleton key_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3030), useful in place of thieves’ tools when attempting to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412). A _shrieking key_ has no activation, however. When you use it to Pick a Lock, the key emits a loud shriek audible for 500 feet despite ambient noise. Physical barriers still block or muffle the shriek as normal. You also take a –2 circumstance penalty to the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check rather than enjoying a bonus. After you attempt such a check with the key the first time, it fuses to you, returning to your possession if discarded. To use another device to Pick a Lock, you must first succeed at a DC 20 Will save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shrieking Key

A silver charm bracelet, a _sluggish bracelet_ appears to be a [_bracelet of dashing_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3060), granting you a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks. If the curse goes unrecognized, you think you can Activate it to gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed for 1 minute. Instead, its activation is as follows.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** You take a –10-foot penalty to your Speed for 1 minute, and the bracelet fuses to you. Thereafter, it grants you no bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, and it imposes a –5-foot status penalty to your Speed.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

</row>

</column>

---

Made of a deep-brown rosewood, a _spurned lute_ is adorned with carved flowers. The lute appears to be and functions as a [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737). (Other spurned instruments exist, but the lute is the least rare.) This lute has a jealous streak, demanding total loyalty from its “partner” musician. After you play the lute for the first time, it fuses to you. If you go a day without using it to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), you become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until you next do so. After that, when you attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check using an instrument other than the lute, you take a –4 circumstance penalty to do so, and you must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or become stupefied 1 for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Tattletale Orb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4919)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _tattletale orb_ is a polished crystal sphere that appears to function as a [crystal ball](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3019). If those whom you use the orb to [scry](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) on roll better than a critical failure on their saving throw, they receive a telepathic message alerting them to the scrying. A success or better at the save allows the target to choose to allow you to scry anyway, knowing they can use an aspect of the orb against you, according to the orb’s type. A creature that rolls a critical success on the saving throw also learns your name and location. Once you Activate a _tattletale orb_ or use it to cast one of your scrying spells, it fuses to you. You must succeed at a Will save, using the scrying Will DC of a _crystal ball_ of the orb’s type, to use another such device.

_Tattletale orbs_ come in the same types as _crystal balls_, with the same activations and powers. However, your target must roll a critical failure on the saving throw for the orb to function as normal for that type of _crystal ball._

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Clear Quartz)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Selenite)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb's [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) for 24 hours and can choose to be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) to the orb's scrying during that time.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Moonstone)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb’s [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) for 24 hours and can use _mind reading_ against you instead, as if the target used the orb.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Peridot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target can send you one [_telepathic demand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2036).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Obsidian)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb's [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) for 24 hours. During that time, you see a [_false vision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1522) of the target's choice if you scry on the target. Despite being higher level than the spell, you can't disbelieve this illusion if scrying through the orb.
name: Tattletale Orb

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Tattletale Orb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4919)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _tattletale orb_ is a polished crystal sphere that appears to function as a [crystal ball](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3019). If those whom you use the orb to [scry](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) on roll better than a critical failure on their saving throw, they receive a telepathic message alerting them to the scrying. A success or better at the save allows the target to choose to allow you to scry anyway, knowing they can use an aspect of the orb against you, according to the orb’s type. A creature that rolls a critical success on the saving throw also learns your name and location. Once you Activate a _tattletale orb_ or use it to cast one of your scrying spells, it fuses to you. You must succeed at a Will save, using the scrying Will DC of a _crystal ball_ of the orb’s type, to use another such device.

_Tattletale orbs_ come in the same types as _crystal balls_, with the same activations and powers. However, your target must roll a critical failure on the saving throw for the orb to function as normal for that type of _crystal ball._

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Clear Quartz)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Selenite)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb's [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) for 24 hours and can choose to be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) to the orb's scrying during that time.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Moonstone)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb’s [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) for 24 hours and can use _mind reading_ against you instead, as if the target used the orb.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Peridot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target can send you one [_telepathic demand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2036).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tattletale Orb (Obsidian)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The target is temporarily immune to the orb's [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) for 24 hours. During that time, you see a [_false vision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1522) of the target's choice if you scry on the target. Despite being higher level than the spell, you can't disbelieve this illusion if scrying through the orb.
name: Tattletale Orb (Selenite)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Unfathomable Stargazer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4920)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _unfathomable stargazer_ is a handheld brass telescope etched with constellations, the patterns of which form bizarre, shifting runes. You can observe the night sky with an _unfathomable stargazer_, however, the constellations you glimpse through the telescope distort with expanding and contracting fields of darkness among them. You use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) instead of [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) or [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) when observing the skies with this item, and gain a +2 item bonus on all checks to do so. Once you use it, it fuses to you, and you must succeed at a DC 35 Will save to use another device to observe the stars, including your naked eyes. The telescope imposes a –4 circumstance penalty to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) or navigate. If you critically fail this Survival check, you’re subjected to [_warp mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) (DC 34) as you glimpse something horrifying and alien amid the darkness.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unfathomable Stargazer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Arboreal Staff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4921)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Staff" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=151" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 193

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +25; precise vision ([low-light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411)) 60 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet, imprecise [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) 30 feet (only when touching the ground)

**Communication** speech ([Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +22, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +25

**Int** +4, **Wis** +6, **Cha** +4

**Will** +27 

</column>

---        

Rather than passing on when they feel their death approaching, a rare few elderly [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=364) undergo a ritual of preservation. This rite fuses the arboreal’s consciousness into a branch, allowing their spirit to live on within the object. The branch functions as a [_greater verdant staff_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3049), though the arboreal spirit allows only partners willing to safeguard nature to use the staff’s spells. An arboreal who decides to become an _arboreal staff_ is more connected to shorter-lived species than others of their kind. Most choose to endure to share their knowledge with future generations, but they take a long view in ways that can be frustrating to their wielders. 

In addition to the activation and spells of a _greater verdant staff_, an _arboreal staff_ can cast the spells it holds as if it were the staff’s wielder, though it usually does so only to benefit a partner. The staff also has [_earthbind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) available as a 3rd-level spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Arboreal Staff

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Bleachguard Doll](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4922)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 194

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +16; precise vision ([low-light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411)) 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech (one language spoken by the doll’s creator)

**Skills** One Intelligence-, Wisdom-, or Charisma-based skill chosen by the creator at +16

**Int** +3, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +3

**Will** +16 

</column>

---        

Rather than seeking out new experiences to stave off the Bleaching, [gnome](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=61) spellcasters with the right know-how can infuse a small fragment of their consciousness into a doll, then send the doll off with a crew of adventurers to experience new things. Such gnomes might have difficulties that keep them from exploring or might be preoccupied with other pursuits, but the doll acts as an intermediary, transmitting the benefits of new experiences to its creator via a potent, but passive, psychic link. A _bleachguard doll_ has the alignment and demeanor of its creator, but new experiences can cause the two to diverge. The doll’s creator is neither able to control it nor are they constantly aware of its activities. If the original creator perishes, the doll sometimes becomes inert (essentially dying as well), sometimes remains an intelligent item, and sometimes becomes a normal [soulbound doll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3204), diminished in power but with some of the item’s capabilities.

Once per day, the doll sends its creator a dream of the day’s events. Each _bleachguard doll_ can cast the cantrips [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) (3rd level), [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), and one other 3rd-level cantrip, along with one spell (at 3rd level) that can be cast once per day. The creator chooses the cantrip and spells, which can’t require an attack roll or saving throw, during the doll’s creation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, interact;     **Effect** The _bleachguard doll_ casts one of its available cantrips or spells.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _bleachguard doll_ animates per [_summon construct_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1696) as a [soulbound doll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3204), retaining pertinent item statistics, cantrips, and its spell instead of those of the soulbound doll. The soulbound doll can Cast a Spell only if the _bleachguard doll_ has a use of that spell remaining for the day. When reduced to 0 Hit Points or this activation ends, the _bleachguard doll_ becomes inert but repairs itself at the rate of 1 Hit Point per hour. This activation is unavailable until the doll has 23 Hit Points again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bleachguard Doll

Prayer beads given prolonged exposure to spiritual energies at sacred [Sarenite](/Deities.aspx?ID=292) sites can attain sapience as _Dawnflower beads_. Other such objects hold the spirits of Sarenite priests who dedicated themselves to their work beyond death. _Dawnflower beads_ function as [_greater holy prayer beads_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=256). They don’t re-attune to other deities but allow any [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) or neutral divine spellcaster to use them, though they attempt to talk their wielder out of morally questionable acts. Add [_positive luminance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=439) to the beads’ spell list, available to be cast once per day. _Dawnflower beads_ also have the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;     **Effect** The beads cast 4th-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585).

A suit of [_autumn's embrace_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1840) armor can gain sapience when lovingly crafted by a fae monarch of sufficient power; a suit of _faerie queen's bower_ is one such example. The armor is happy to give you and your companions counsel, hoping to guide you on a path of benevolence and aid you in battles against forces that seek to cause harm to the natural world. It refuses access to its magic to anyone who causes undue harm to plants or animals.

In addition to the features and activation of _autumn's embrace_, _faerie queen's bower_ has the following activation. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor casts 4th-level [_safe passage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659), with the protected area beginning from your square and extending to a place of relative safety.

</column>

**Int** +4, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +1

**Will** +11

---        

A colorful feather adorns an _inquisitive quill_, which never runs out of ink. Essentially a Tiny construct, an _inquisitive quill_ can stand on its own when given a surface, balancing as it writes or stands. (Drawing a line gives it an effective Speed of 5 feet.) It has strong curiosity, eagerly writing any information offered to it, often acting as a scribe for its partner. The quill copies text and drawings it can see or transcribes dictation it hears. It writes at a rate that rivals that of an expert scribe.

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +17; precise vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet, imprecise sense of heat 30 feet

**Communication** telepathy ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18), and two other common languages)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +21, [Fire Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +23, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17

**Will** +17 

</column>

---        

A _kindled tome_ is a [_book of lingering blazes_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=990) awakened to sapience and imbued with an enthusiasm for fire. It encourages you to learn new fire spells to inscribe within its pages. When you attempt a skill check to [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2366) to add a spell with the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait to the kindled tome, you treat your result as one step better than you rolled. The tome can be disparaging about other spells as “a waste of good page space,” especially if those spells have the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) traits. In addition to the activation of a _book of lingering blazes_, a kindled tome has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You Cast a spell with the fire trait;  **Effect** The tome makes the flames linger, dealing 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to all you hit with or who fail the saving throw against the effect (doubling on a critical success on a spell attack and on a critical failure on a saving throw).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tome casts [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) at 5th level to your specifications.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) and 1 imparted language)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +13, [Nobility Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +13

**Int** +3, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +3

**Will** +14 

</column>

---        

Forged in platinum, a _majordomo torc_ is engraved with heraldic insignias along with one language’s alphabet, much like a choker of elocution. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks and the ability to understand, speak, and write the torc’s language. Your excellent elocution reduces the DC of the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to perform an auditory action while [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) from 5 to 3. 

Upon being invested, the torc appoints itself your majordomo and, given the chance, takes over coordinating your social calendar, engagements, and wardrobe. A _majordomo torc_ has a prim, fussy disposition, and although it defers to you, it can grow sardonic if you frequently ignore its advice. The torc has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _majordomo torc_ casts [_befitting attire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=867) on you, usually to your specifications. However the torc can also choose the appearance of the [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) for you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Majordomo Torc

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 196 **Hands** 2 **Bulk** 16 --- **Perception** +22; precise vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet **Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (any languages the possessing spirit knew in life) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +23, [Music Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +23, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +21 **Int** +3, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +5 **Will** +22 --- When a skilled musician dies before reaching their full potential or accomplishing an important goal, their soul might refuse to move on to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39). Rather than becoming [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), the spirit possesses an instrument they played in life, turning it into an intelligent item and a [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737). (Other phantom instruments exist, but most are heavy.) A _phantom piano_ offers a bargain to any [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=32) or other skilled musician who finds it: receive musical aid as you dedicate yourself to reaching the success the spirit felt they were denied in life. The piano refuses to play for those who reject this deal. If you accept, the piano asks only that you practice with it and perform for increasingly refined and august audiences, requiring a successful [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check appropriate to your proficiency level. The piano sets no strict timeline, but at the GM’s discretion, if you go too long without making progress, the piano can cease cooperating with you until it’s satisfied with your dedication once again. While you keep up your end of the bargain, the piano grants you a +2 item bonus to Performance checks made with it. The piano also provides access to composition spells based on your Performance proficiency rank, as if you had the corresponding feat. The spells you can use comprise a cumulative list, but you can cast them only when using the piano. You must spend the actions and Focus Points required to cast these spells. **Trained:** [_inspire competence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=385), [_inspire courage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=386)
**Expert:** [_hymn of healing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1768), [_inspire defense_](/Spells.aspx?ID=387)
**Master:** [_symphony of the unfettered heart_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1777)
**Legendary:** [_soothing ballad_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1776) A _phantom piano_ has the following activations. **Activate** command; **Effect** The piano shrinks down to a figurine of light Bulk compact enough to store in a pouch, or from figurine form back to instrument form. In figurine form, the piano doesn’t function as an instrument or provide magic other than this activation. **Activate** command; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) the piano provides you.
name: Phantom Piano

>
[Purloining Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4930)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 196

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +30; precise vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) and two other languages, usually regional and ancestral)

**Skills** [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +31, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +31, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +31

**Int** +5, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +5

**Will** +30 

</column>

---        

Each _purloining cloak_ is a mercurial mantle once worn by a legendary thief or two. The cloak became infused with a daredevil spirit and a penchant for high-stakes thievery. If its owner isn’t willing to partake in such activities, the cloak tries to convince them to sell or give it to someone who will. A _purloining cloak_ can use tendrils of its cloth to attempt [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks to [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=2409) or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410), proudly offering you its loot later. In addition to those of a [_mercurial mantle_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1067), a _purloining cloak_ has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;     **Effect** The cloak casts 2nd-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) on you, usually to your specifications. However the cloak can choose the appearance of the illusion for you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The cloak assesses the price of valuables it can see. This valuation doesn’t include the value of features the cloak can’t discern, such as magical properties.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Purloining Cloak

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (Common and two other common languages)

**Skills** [Clockwork Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +13, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +11

**Int** +4, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +0

**Will** +12 

</column>

---        

Sacred to the faith of [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=333), a _toolkit of bronze whispers_ has been used with such devotion it has developed a consciousness and personality that can be a blessing to a partnered crafter. A _toolkit of bronze whispers_ functions as a set of [sterling artisan’s tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703). Such toolkits focus on new creations and enthusiastically suggest crafting techniques to wielders. A _toolkit of bronze whispers_ knows common item formulas of its level or lower and any other formulas the GM chooses. It can teach these formulas. You must still spend the time to copy a formula before you can use it.

</column>

    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Toshigami Blossom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4932)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 197

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +30; precise vision ([darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)) 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) and two other common languages; [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +28, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +32

**Int** +6, **Wis** +10, **Cha** +5

**Will** +32 

</column>

---        

An encounter with a [toshigami](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1204), the enigmatic [kami](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=604) who protect cherry trees, is rare, though often sought after and treasured by those who achieve such an encounter. Only a handful of mortals can truthfully claim to have seen a toshigami, let alone met one, though many popular fireside tales tell of virtuous souls receiving a toshigami’s blessing to fight for a worthy cause. Such stories have a basis in fact; every so often, a toshigami gives a worthy mortal a flower from their ward, time-locked in perfect bloom and granted sapience. Such _toshigami blossoms_ are more sociable than their creators. Like toshigami, a blossom has a strong curiosity about the mortal world. If you wear a _toshigami blossom_, it can intercede for you, helping you make a good impression.

A _toshigami blossom_ has the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The blossom casts [_tree stride_](/Spells.aspx?ID=343) on you to your specifications. If you target only cherry trees, the spell is cast at 6th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The blossom casts [_soothing blossoms_](/Spells.aspx?ID=999) to your specifications.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The blossom sends a flurry of cherry blossoms outward in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) that lasts 1 round. You and your allies can see through these blossoms. To all other creatures, creatures within the cloud of blossoms become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it. When you or an ally succeeds with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a creature in the blossoms, the Strike deals an additional 1d6 mental damage and an additional 1d6 negative damage to living creatures, or an additional 1d6 positive damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Toshigami Blossom

>
[Wrestler's Armbands](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4933)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 197

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armbands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +17; precise vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) and one other common or regional language)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Sports Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17

**Int** +2, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +4

**Will** +17 

</column>

---        

_Wrestler’s armbands_ are a set of armbands of athleticism that have gained their own boisterous sapience. Often, such items aren’t created intentionally but rather are the result of a truly charismatic champion whose heart and body are unusually strong wearing the armbands of athleticism on many adventures as their most important item. Eventually, the essence of their earnest and adventurous spirit has so thoroughly inundated the item it gains a matching spirit and will of its own. Such armbands view themselves as a tough, but fair, trainer for whomever inherits them next, inspiring you to new heights of athleticism. They also spoil for contests, telepathically calling out competitors on your behalf; typically the armbands won’t challenge a foe you’re unprepared to face, but particularly eager _wrestler’s armbands_ might have an unfortunate habit of overestimating your abilities and pushing you into a conflict that’s beyond your proven capacity. These armbands have the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check;  **Effect** The armbands taunt a creature you’re competing with, boasting of your prowess and attempting an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) that creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wrestler's Armbands

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 204 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- The _fan of the four winds_ once belonged to a sage of [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=284) and was passed down as a sacred relic for generations to those found worthy. Since that time, no one has unlocked the fan’s full powers, though many devout priests have used it to aid them in their travels. A worthy member of this order might receive the fan as a reward, as could a suitable helper of the clergy. The fan might also have been lost, allowing for a new wielder to find it. The _fan of the four winds_ has the following activation. **Activate** command, interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You fan in a direction, and the fan casts [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550). If this casting is directed toward the sail of a vessel, it instead fills it with swirling air. The vessel gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. --- **Minor Gifts**: [feather steps](/Relics.aspx?ID=2), [wind barrier](/Relics.aspx?ID=3)
**Major Gifts**: [lightning storm](/Relics.aspx?ID=4), [propelling winds](/Relics.aspx?ID=5)
**Grand Gift**: [living storm](/Relics.aspx?ID=6)
name: Fan of the Four Winds

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 204 **Usage** worn --- Blessed by [Nivi Rhombodazzle](/Deities.aspx?ID=354), this striking silver necklace is adorned with sparkling sapphires that draw the eye of any who see it. Over the years, this necklace has appeared in many paintings and illustrations, usually around the neck of a carefree and daring adventurer or a stylish rake gambling vast sums of coin. Finding this relic is always seen as a sign of great fortune to come, but when that luck runs out, the necklace is lost just as quickly. Wearing the relic, you quickly realize how distracting it can be, granting you a +1 item bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks made to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434) and on [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks made to gamble or determine the outcome of a game of chance. --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [whammy](/Relics.aspx?ID=99)
**Major Gifts**: [clean luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=100), [perception filter](/Relics.aspx?ID=52)
**Grand Gift**: [against all odds](/Relics.aspx?ID=102)
name: Fortune's Favor

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A famed detective once owned the long, curved _sleuth’s pipe_, taking it with them on investigations, keeping it in hand or mouth while cogitating. The pipe might have passed down through generations. Or, when an owner dies without heirs, it could go up for auction, be given to family friends, or remain at the scene of a crime to be found later. The _sleuth’s pipe_ has sapience that eventually awakens, allowing the relic to act as a helpful assistant in investigations. Even while this sapience sleeps within the pipe, it grants you a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302), and if you’re an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59), any checks you make to [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=2811). --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [repository of knowledge](/Relics.aspx?ID=51)
**Major Gifts**: [intelligent relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=109), [steal luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=101)
**Grand Gift**: [mental bastion](/Relics.aspx?ID=54)
name: Sleuth's Pipe

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- The origins of the _watch of lost ages_, a silver pocket watch, are enigmatic. Its construction matches no known culture, and its appearance shifts back and forth from battered and tarnished to shiny and new without cause or apparent reason. The watch exudes a sense of melancholy and loss, and anyone holding it is acutely aware of the impermanence of all things and the march of time, moving ever forward. This relic might show up in a mysterious ruin or spontaneously appear in a spellcaster’s hand after they cast a [time](/Traits.aspx?ID=712) spell. The relic grants you a +1 item bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about historical subjects. --- **Minor Gifts**: [fervor](/Relics.aspx?ID=93), [sands of the hourglass](/Relics.aspx?ID=104)
**Major Gifts**: [fervent spell](/Relics.aspx?ID=95), [temporal pulse](/Relics.aspx?ID=106)
**Grand Gift**: [time reversal](/Relics.aspx?ID=108)
name: Watch of Lost Ages

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 169 --- A durable material when properly treated, bone can replace wood and metal when creating armor, shields, and weapons. The statistics for bone are applicable to a variety of other sturdy animal-based products, such as chitin, horn, ivory, coral, and shell, which can also be classified as bone for the purposes of determining item statistics. Bone elements from certain beasts, like the horn of a [unicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=397) or the spikes of a [manticore](/Monsters.aspx?ID=294), may even yield magical properties when worked by a skilled crafter. ## Bone Objects

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 169 --- Stone was one of the earliest materials used to create tools, and crafters can still fashion it into a variety of implements, weapons, and even armor. Simple stone objects are made from common materials such as flint or basalt, while exquisite stone objects might be made from crystal, obsidian, or semi-precious gems. Particularly notable stone armors or weapons might even be crafted entirely from a giant emerald, ruby, or sapphire, though they can still use the base statistics presented below. Stone can replace the striking surface of any melee weapon as long as it has no complex moving parts, and ammunition can be crafted with it. Stone can replace metal components in [chain](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=1) and [composite](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=2) armor; only [plate](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=5) armor that specifically describes itself as being made from stone can be crafted from this material ## Stone Objects
**Hardness** **HP** **BT**
Thin Items 4 16 8
Items 7 24 12
Structures 14 48 24
 Stone Object (Low-Grade) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 169 **Price** 1 sp (per Bulk)

May contain spoilers from Troubles in Grayce **Source** [Troubles in Grayce](/Sources.aspx?ID=327) pg. 117 **Price** 75 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- This simple doll is made with scraps of cloth tied or sewn together into a basic humanoid shape, but its appearance belies its strange power over the actions of others. The head and limbs of the doll constantly droop and sway slightly, never able to remain in one position for very long. Whoever holds a _floppy rag doll_ can feel it squirming in their grasp. **Activate—Loose Limbs** [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You brandish the _floppy rag doll_ at a creature within 30 feet who can see you and shake it about. The creature's own limbs begin moving wildly in a similar fashion, and they must attempt a DC 19 Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target can't use reactions until the end your turn.
**Failure** The target is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and can't use reactions until the beginning of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but clumsy 2.
name: Floppy Rag Doll

This rose-shaped crystal brooch is a translucent bloodred. These talismans have saved the lives of many a soldier on the field of battle. When you activate a _guardian rose_, you avoid being knocked out and regain 2d8+5 Hit Points. However, this puts a strain on your body and you are [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) until the end of your next turn.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs=""
>
[Last Word Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4944)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Troubles in Grayce</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Troubles in Grayce](/Sources.aspx?ID=327) pg. 117

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small, palm-sized smooth crystal has a translucent gray coloration. But with a whisper from the lips of the desperate, a _last word stone_ can be filled with a spark of memory, changing its appearance to an opaque, polished stone that is strangely warm to the touch. These divinely infused objects were initially created by [Pharasmin](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) priests to preserve a dying person's last moment of life should loved ones be delayed in their arrival at the dying person's side. But with the tragedy of Lastwall, _last word stones_ have gained a new, more desperate purpose. Here, along the Gravelands border regions, refugees and adventurers have taken to using _last word stones_ to impart important details to recipients outside of endangered places when it seems likely they won't survive to spread the news themselves.

**Activate—Capture Memory** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)   **Requirements** The _last word stone_ doesn't contain a memory; **Effect** You press an empty _last word stone_ to an adjacent willing creature's head. The stone fills with an important memory from the creature's mind, then becomes opaque in appearance. The memory is duplicated, and remains in the target creature's thoughts, but no indication of what type of memory is held within the stone is discernible to anyone else. Which memory is placed in the _last word stone_ is chosen by the creature from whom the memory is copied, and once a memory is stored, the stone can't be used again to Capture Memory.

 A stored memory can't contain much information beyond a single emotion and a hazy mental picture that, taken together, convey a short message of no more than 10 words— typically enough to impart final words of comfort to a loved one, or to warn one of a specific location that might be trapped, or to impart a bit of news about a noteworthy event. The limit of 10 words represents the hazy and sometimes confusing nature of the mental image.

**Activate—Reveal Memory** <actions string="Three Actions" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)   **Requirements** The _last word stone_ contains a memory; **Effect** You hold the _last word stone_ to your head and concentrate on its contents. A moment later, the stored memory plays out in your mind and the _last word stone_ crumbles to dust in your hand.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Last Word Stone

<title

    right="Item 2+"
    pfs=""
>
[Lucky Coin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4945)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Troubles in Grayce</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Troubles in Grayce](/Sources.aspx?ID=327) pg. 118

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These coins—copper, silver, or gold depending on their potency—were not minted for circulation. Rather, friendly fey helped to create them as wards against the abilities of some [gremlins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=424), namely [pugwampis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3032). The coins are said to protect the bearer against misfortunes of all stripes, though, not merely those of gremlins and have come to be seen as good luck charms the world over. Each coin, regardless of the metal used to mint it, has a canine face on both sides; on one side, the face's mouth is open in a wide smile, while the other shows the face snarling.

**Activate—Guarded Fortune** <actions string="Free Action" /> [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)  **Trigger** You fail a skill check due to a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect;  **Effect** Flip the coin. If it lands on the smiling face, reroll the triggering check; since this activation has the [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect, any misfortune effect is canceled out for this check. You must use the new result, even if it's worse. If it lands on the snarling face, nothing happens. Either way, you're temporarily immune to Guarded Fortune for 1 hour.

**Craft Requirements** Any checks made to craft this item while under a misfortune effect are automatic critical failures.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs=""
>
[Lucky Coin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4945)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Troubles in Grayce</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Troubles in Grayce](/Sources.aspx?ID=327) pg. 118

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These coins—copper, silver, or gold depending on their potency—were not minted for circulation. Rather, friendly fey helped to create them as wards against the abilities of some [gremlins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=424), namely [pugwampis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3032). The coins are said to protect the bearer against misfortunes of all stripes, though, not merely those of gremlins and have come to be seen as good luck charms the world over. Each coin, regardless of the metal used to mint it, has a canine face on both sides; on one side, the face's mouth is open in a wide smile, while the other shows the face snarling.

**Activate—Guarded Fortune** <actions string="Free Action" /> [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)  **Trigger** You fail a skill check due to a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect;  **Effect** Flip the coin. If it lands on the smiling face, reroll the triggering check; since this activation has the [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect, any misfortune effect is canceled out for this check. You must use the new result, even if it's worse. If it lands on the snarling face, nothing happens. Either way, you're temporarily immune to Guarded Fortune for 1 hour.

**Craft Requirements** Any checks made to craft this item while under a misfortune effect are automatic critical failures.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs=""
>
[Sweet Hag's Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4946)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Troubles in Grayce</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=689" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Troubles in Grayce](/Sources.aspx?ID=327) pg. 118

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This item appears to be a normal, if admittedly macabre, piece of hard peppermint candy the size and shape of a human eyeball. That's because it is actually the eye of a [sweet hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3041), removed and painstakingly replaced with a mix of enchantment and cookery. It is typically mounted on a piece of jewelry or stored in a small sack worn on a cord.

 The eye provides no direct benefit for the wearer, but the hag whose eye it once was can peer through it using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action, and cast the [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) cantrip through it onto the creature carrying the eye. Both these effects have no range limited, provided the hag is on the same plane.

 Any damage to the eye destroys it. The sweet hag can also choose to destroy the eye remotely using Meltaway Mint. The eye can also be eaten, but a creature who attempts this is exposed to sickeningly sweet. 

**Activate—Meltaway Mint** <actions string="Single Action" /> ;     **Effect** The hag causes the eye to melt away under a brief flare of intense heat. If a creature is holding the eye, they must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. On a failure, they take 1d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) as the boiling hot candy remains stick to and burn their skin.

 **Sickeningly Sweet** ([ingested](/Traits.aspx?ID=633), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) condition caused by this poison can't be ended until the poison's effects end; **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 10 minutes; **Stage 1** sickened 1 (1 minute); **Stage 2** 1d8 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 minute); **Stage 3** 2d8 poison damage and sickened 3 (1 minute)

**Craft Requirements** You must be a sweet hag.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sweet Hag's Eye

A humanoid shape has been cut out of a single sheet of colored paper with a face and clothes drawn on. The face looks slightly worried, as if the doll knows what it was made for. That creature takes 3d6 slashing damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) as the tears of the paper doll are mimicked on its body.

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs=""
>
[Vagabond's Teapot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4948)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Troubles in Grayce</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Troubles in Grayce](/Sources.aspx?ID=327) pg. 118

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These ceramic teapots are highly valued by travelers. While the teapot is filled with water, a creature can use a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait to instantly heat the water to boiling, at which point the kettle whistles and the water inside begins to cool at a normal rate. While the water remains heated, it is piping hot, and a creature splashed with it takes 1d4 [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) fire damage. Instead of filling the teapot with water, you can pour an [elixir](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=8) of 4th level or lower into the pot and use the following activation.

**Activate—Share Brew** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The teapot magically increases the volume of the inserted elixir to produce four uses of the same elixir. Once an elixir has been brewed in the teapot in this way, further attempts that day to brew another elixir spoil the inserted liquid. Any brewed elixir not consumed by the time it cools (typically 10 minutes) loses its alchemical properties.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vagabond's Teapot

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Banner of Common Cause](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4952)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=542" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 225

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Embroidered with symbols of unity, this banner inspires even the meekest warrior to step up and lead when circumstances demand. 

**Activate—Pass the Banner** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You’re knocked [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95); **Requirements** You’re leading a troop; **Effect** A member of the troop you’re leading picks up and displays the banner, maintaining the troop’s cohesion. When attempting its rout check due to losing its leader, the troop rolls its Will save twice and uses the better result. 

**Activate—Stand Firm** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A frightened ally begins their turn within your banner’s aura; **Requirements** ; **Effect** Reduce the ally’s [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Banner of Common Cause

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 21"
    pfs=""
>
[The Eye of the Slumberer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4953)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 225

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** — (see below)

**Bulk** 250
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _Eye of the Slumberer_ is an [alghollthu](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=358) relic resembling a tangle of barnacle-encrusted tentacles. Though the orb is too large to move easily, the artifact’s owner (referred to as the bearer) can activate its powers by wearing the _Amulet of the Slumberer_, a companion item shaped like a barnacle-encrusted cephalopod eye; this is a worn item that has the invested and occult traits. Only one such amulet can exist at any given time, and the artifact can grow a new one in 10 minutes if the original is destroyed. The _Eye’s_ abilities that require a saving throw use the higher of the amulet wearer’s spell DC or class DC. 

As one of the alghollthus’ ancient contingencies to control their servitors, the _Eye_ absorbs thoughts—especially those of pain and anguish—storing them like a battery. On command, it can unleash the stored energy, potentially stunning whole ecosystems. Each time a creature with the aquatic, amphibious, or water trait falls asleep or otherwise passes the night within 1 mile of the _Eye of the Slumberer_, it dreams fitfully of the artifact; creatures that don’t dream are immune to this effect. When it awakens, the creature must succeed at a DC 45 Will save or become [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=75) toward the bearer (or [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=78) on a critical failure) until the next time it rests; this is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect. The _Amulet’s_ bearer isn’t automatically aware of creatures influenced in this fashion, but they can identify such creatures on sight. 

**Activate—Open the Eye** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are wearing the _Amulet of the Slumberer_ and are within 100 feet of the _Eye of the Slumberer_; **Effect** The amulet’s eye opens and absorbs fears from creatures within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), subjecting them to [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) as if they’d spent a night under the influence of the spell. Additionally, each creature with the [dream](/Traits.aspx?ID=582) or [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) trait within 500 feet of the _Eye of the Slumberer_ takes 20d6 force damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). 

**Activate—Vortex** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), water)   **Requirements** You’re wearing the _Amulet of the Slumberer_, you’re within 100 feet of the _Eye of the Slumberer,_ and the _Eye_ is within water; **Effect** The [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) around the _Eye_ churns violently in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for swimming creatures that don’t have the water trait. You can use this action again to [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect.

**Destruction** The _Eye of the Slumberer_ is destroyed if it’s brought to the [Dreamlands](/Planes.aspx?ID=47) and either fed to a [Great Old One](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13) or encircled by joyful celebrants for 101 days, during which time none of the dancers can experiences despair.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Eye of the Slumberer

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action or One Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** You don’t have the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait.</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

---

This pristine feather is plucked from a mighty celestial, such as Talmandor. When you activate the talisman, you sprout feathery wings until the end of your turn, granting you a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 50 feet and a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against reactions triggered by your movement. You then [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312). If you activate the talisman with 1 action, the wings buffet adjacent enemies either before or after you Fly, dealing 8d8 spirit damage (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Golden Liberator's Feather

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 226

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This iron thurible—originally crafted by a [Caydenite](/Deities.aspx?ID=280) priest to disperse symbolic libations—swings from the end of an extendable shaft, allowing you to sprinkle the contents over an entire troop. 

The thurible’s container can hold a magical potion up to the item level of the _warpriest’s cascade_, dispersing its contents over an entire troop over the course of 1 minute. However, spreading out the potion lessens its duration. Potions with a duration of 1 hour or more affect a troop for only 10 minutes, and potions with a duration of 8 hours or more affect a troop for only 1 hour. Potions with a duration less than 1 hour don’t work inside of a _warpriest’s cascade_.

[Cloak of Social Graces](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4957)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These cloaks are created from hand-dyed leather decorated with gemstones, appealing to the individual tastes of the owner. The beautiful decorations of these cloaks encourage their owner to wear them no matter the season, making it visibly part of their unique style. Wearing the cloak boosts self-confidence, granting a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks. 

**Activate—Overcome Self-Doubt** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You readjust your cloak, straightening the collar and brushing out any wrinkles. You gain the effects of a _[heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555)_ spell for 1 minute.
