markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloak of Social Graces](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4957)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These cloaks are created from hand-dyed leather decorated with gemstones, appealing to the individual tastes of the owner. The beautiful decorations of these cloaks encourage their owner to wear them no matter the season, making it visibly part of their unique style. Wearing the cloak boosts self-confidence, granting a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks. 

**Activate—Overcome Self-Doubt** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You readjust your cloak, straightening the collar and brushing out any wrinkles. You gain the effects of a _[heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555)_ spell for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cloak of Social Graces
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 380 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Cloak of Social Graces
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and when you activate the cloak, you gain the benefits of a 6th-rank _heroism_ spell for 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Major Cloak of Social Graces
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and when you activate the cloak, you gain the benefits of a 9th-rank _heroism_ spell for 1 minute.
name: Cloak of Social Graces

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gloves of Precision](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4958)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These fingerless gloves increase the agility and sensitivity of the hands, enabling the wearer to perform more delicate, precise work. While wearing the _gloves of precision,_ you gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), and to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) and [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412). 

**Activate—Nimble Fingers** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt a skill check to Climb, Disable a Device, or Pick a Lock;  **Effect** You roll the skill check twice and take the better result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Gloves of Precision
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Gloves of Precision
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The gloves grant a +3 item bonus.
name: Gloves of Precision

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Golden Wings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4959)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pair of huge wings is crafted from numerous feathers made of a lightweight, golden magical alloy. When activated, the wings emit a melodic sound and a shimmering glow. 

**Activate—Assisted Flight** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next 10 minutes, you can [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) 20 feet, using the wings to propel yourself. You must land on a space with solid ground. For the duration, you also emit a [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet. You take a –2 item penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Golden Wings
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Golden Wings
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate the wings, for the duration, you can also use an action to flap the wings to glide slowly toward the ground, 5 feet down and up to 20 feet forward through the air. As long as you spend 1 action gliding each round and haven’t yet reached the ground, you remain in the air at the end of your turn.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Major Golden Wings
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate the wings, you also gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 20 feet for the duration.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
True Golden Wings
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate the wings, you also gain a fly Speed equal to your Speed or 25 feet, whichever is greater, for the duration.
name: Golden Wings

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Golden Wings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4959)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pair of huge wings is crafted from numerous feathers made of a lightweight, golden magical alloy. When activated, the wings emit a melodic sound and a shimmering glow. 

**Activate—Assisted Flight** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next 10 minutes, you can [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) 20 feet, using the wings to propel yourself. You must land on a space with solid ground. For the duration, you also emit a [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet. You take a –2 item penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kinetic Wraps of Strength](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4960)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tightly woven strips strengthen the hands and arms, allowing for the wearer to increase their application of force while lifting heavy loads, and shoving past blockades. Promise sculptors primarily use them to handle large blocks of stone, moving them around as they prepare to carve them. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). 

**Activate—Burst of Might** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt an Athletics check to Force Open, Reposition, or Shove;  **Effect** You roll the skill check twice and take the better result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Kinetic Wraps of Strength
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Kinetic Wraps of Strength
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The wraps grant a +3 item bonus.
name: Kinetic Wraps of Strength

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Bloodstride Blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4961)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=710" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 64

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This item is restricted due to the teleportation effect._

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Sawtooth Saber](/Weapons.aspx?ID=423)
</row>

**Access** You're a member of the Red Mantis Assassins

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [cunning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=885) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) sawtooth saber_ appears rusty but never loses its edge. It’s drawn to the blood of your targets. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) creatures that have blood. 

**Activate—Call of the Bloodthirst** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet takes [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) damage;  **Effect** You cast _[translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)_, appearing adjacent to the triggering creature, then [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) it with this weapon. The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bloodstride Blade

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bloodstride Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4962)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=710" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 64

**Price** 2,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're a member of the Red Mantis Assassins

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These red leather boots greedily absorb any blood they touch. 

**Activate—Blood Hop** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The boots produce a small pool of blood beneath your feet and in a free space you can see within 120 feet. You melt into the pool beneath you and reconstitute at the other pool, with the effects of _[translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)_. 

**Activate—Blood Walk** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re standing in blood; **Effect** You melt into the ground and reconstitute at a location you have previously seen that’s within 100 miles, with the effects of _translocate_. This effect fails if no sentient creature has died at that location in the past 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bloodstride Boots

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Gaze of the Mantis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4963)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 64

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Seeing all Red Mantis contracts is outside the scope of PFS play._

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're a member of the Red Mantis Assassins

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These glasses have faceted red lenses like those of a mantis and are popular as a less ostentatious alternative to a full mask. While wearing the _gaze of the mantis_, your field of view increases to give you better awareness of your surroundings. You gain a +2 item bonus to visual Perception checks and cannot be [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375). 

**Activate—Target Sense** <actions string="Single Action" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re a Red Mantis assassin; **Effect** You learn the distance to and direction of all creatures that have a Red Mantis contract on them within 10 miles. If one of these creatures is within 100 feet, you also know any afflictions, conditions, and spells affecting them. This awareness lasts for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gaze of the Mantis

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sanguine Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4964)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 64

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

**Access** You're a member of the Red Mantis Assassins

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wickedly barbed ammunition looks dangerous to even touch. It dissolves into blood on impact, leaving no trace if the target has blood. The target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against diseases and poisons for 1 round. 

You can also [Activate](/Actions.aspx?ID=100) _sanguine ammunition_ as part of [Casting a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734). The spell must be 3rd-rank or lower, must take 1 or 2 actions to cast, must have the [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) trait, and must target a creature other than the caster. Casting a Spell this way has no effect; instead, a creature hit by _sanguine ammunition_ activated this way is targeted by the spell in addition to the item’s normal effects. If the target isn’t a valid target for the spell, the spell is lost. 

The ammunition affects only the target hit. If the spell requires a spell attack, use the result of your ranged attack roll to determine the degree of success of the spell. If the spell requires a save, the target attempts the save against your spell DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sanguine Ammunition

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 64

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) No duration on effects._

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Kusarigama](/Weapons.aspx?ID=291)
</row>

**Access** You're a member of the Red Mantis Assassins

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The serrated blade and chain of this _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) kusarigama_ shine blood red. A crimson haze wafts from the blade. 

**Activate—Ensnaring Disarm** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;   **Trigger** An enemy within reach misses you with a melee weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167);  **Effect** You attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) the triggering creature. You can do so even if you don’t have a hand free. 

**Activate—Bloodmist Whirl** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You whirl the chain of the _sawtooth reaper_, creating a crimson fog that conceals you for 1 round or until you Strike. The next Strike you make treats the target as [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and when you use Ensnaring Disarm you can also Strike the same target as a free action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sawtooth Reaper

>
[Amaranthine Visage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4967)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 78

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from the Mordant Spire

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The shape of this 2-foot-tall wooden mask, worn by the secretive leaders of the elves who inhabit the Mordant Spire, mirrors the tower itself. While wearing it, your innards turn to purple smoke, giving you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 to precision damage. 

**Activate—Eternal Renewal** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition would reach the value that would cause you to die;  **Effect** You Sublimate Self. You lose the dying condition, gaining or increasing your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition as normal, and gain [fast healing](/Rules.aspx?ID=2322) 20 for 1 minute. You can use Eternal Renewal even while [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) or otherwise unable to act. 

**Activate—Sublimate Self** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (air, [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670))    **Effect** Your body transforms completely into purple vapor for 5 minutes, with the effects of _[vapor form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738)_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Amaranthine Visage

    right="Item 4"

[Coral Aspect](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from the Mordant Spire

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of petrified wood, this mask evokes the form of a coral reef. When you wear it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), and any armor you’re wearing gains the [aquadynamic](/Traits.aspx?ID=792) trait. 

**Activate—Cohabitate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 minute, your body becomes a mobile coral reef, letting much smaller creatures pass through or shelter in it. Creatures at least one size category smaller than you can share your space. Allies that share your space gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC, and you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to any damage caused by a creature sharing the same space as you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Coral Aspect

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

<column gap="tiny">

---

The swirls on this wooden mask—often worn by Mordant Censors—move and shift, reflecting the shape of the nearest currents. Wearing the mask grants you a +1 item bonus to [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks. In addition, it allows you to pilot a boat with simply a thought. When you pilot a vehicle, you can keep both hands free and [Drive](/Actions.aspx?ID=3517) from any location on the vehicle. 

**Activate—Set the Course** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are aboard a vehicle; **Effect** You become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute or until you leave the vehicle, whichever comes first, and can use the extra action to [Take Control](/Actions.aspx?ID=3520) of the vehicle or, if you’re already piloting it, Drive or [Stop](/Actions.aspx?ID=3519) the vehicle.

When you pilot a vehicle, you can keep both hands free and [Drive](/Actions.aspx?ID=3517) from any location on the vehicle. 

**Activate—Set the Course** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are aboard a vehicle; **Effect** You become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute or until you leave the vehicle, whichever comes first, and can use the extra action to [Take Control](/Actions.aspx?ID=3520) of the vehicle or, if you’re already piloting it, Drive or [Stop](/Actions.aspx?ID=3519) the vehicle.

A constant murmuring, like waves washing up on the shore, fills your ears when you don this wooden mask. The soothing sounds grant you a +1 item bonus to your Will save and Will DC against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects. 

**Activate—Pounding Surf** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would be affected by an auditory effect;  **Effect** The noise of the waves grow to an overwhelming, crashing booming, drowning out all other sounds. You become [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) to all other noise until the end of your next turn. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the activation up to 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Whispering Veil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4971)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 78

**Price** 2,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from the Mordant Spire

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved concentric circles surround the eye and ear holes on this dark wooden mask, which lets you hear the whispers that fill the Mordant Spire. The barely audible susurrus that fills your ears advises you of peril, granting you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks for initiative and to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), [Search](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630), or [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) against things that intend harm to you, at the GM’s discretion, such as hostile creatures and haunts. If you tie a foe’s initiative roll, you go first. 

**Activate—Spire Power** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are about to attempt a check that your mythic calling would allow you to spend a Mythic Point on;  **Effect** You draw on the power of the Mordant Spire. You gain 1 Mythic Point, which you can spend to attempt the check. If you don’t spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost. You do not regain a Mythic Point if you critically succeed on the check, even if it’s the first time you did so that day, but still do so on the subsequent critical success, as usual. 

**Activate—Unforeseen Alert** <actions string="Free Action" /> ;    **Requirements** A creature or hazard you aren’t aware of targets you or includes you in an area effect, and you aren’t [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66); **Effect** The whispers warn you of the impending danger. You immediately gain a reaction that you can use only to respond to the triggering action or effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whispering Veil

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 108 **Price** 5,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- These trapezoidal crystals were created by a powerful [alghollthu master](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2) from long-forgotten deep sea stones. They were used to steal the dreams and memories of the master’s victims, trapping them inside so they could be consulted when needed. When broken, the creatures whose memories were stolen retrieve them but are [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round. **Activate—Steal Remembrance** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Choose a creature you can see, trapping its memories in the crystal. The creature must attempt a DC 31 Will saving throw. If the creature is asleep, it uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and realizes you tried to steal its memories.
**Success** The creature is stupefied 1 for 1 round but doesn’t lose any memory, and believes you did something harmless to its mind.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 2 for 1 hour and loses all memory of a particular topic that can be detailed in 50 words or fewer.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 4 for 1 day and loses all memory of two particular topics, each of which can be detailed in 50 words or fewer.
name: Oneiric Crystals of the Slumberer

This _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) trident_ is made of golden metal, its handle carved with reliefs of waves and seashells. These tridents are often wielded by high-ranking [azarketi](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=367) soldiers in the ranks of [alghollthu](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=358) armies. 

**Activate—Force of the Sea** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast a 5th-rank _[hydraulic torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562)_. The spell DC is 29.

This item is the starter kit for an adventurer, containing the essential items for exploration and survival. The Bulk value is for the entire pack together, but see the descriptions of individual items as necessary.

 The pack contains the following items: [backpack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4) (containing the other goods), [bedroll](/Equipment.aspx?ID=7), [10 pieces of chalk](/Equipment.aspx?ID=12), [flint and steel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=21), [50 feet of rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44), [2 weeks' rations](/Equipment.aspx?ID=40), [soap](/Equipment.aspx?ID=52), [5 torches](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60), and a [waterskin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=62).

You need these tools to create items from raw materials with the [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) skill. Different sets are needed for different work, as determined by the GM; for example, blacksmith's tools differ from woodworker's tools. If you wear your artisan's tools, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them. 

You can use a set of tools with 1 hand if you're [wearing them](/Rules.aspx?ID=1456) or 2 if you're holding them.

>
[Caltrops](/Equipment.aspx?ID=9)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 287

**Price** 3 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These four-pronged metal spikes can cause damage to a creature's feet. You can scatter caltrops in an empty square adjacent to you with an Interact action. The first creature that moves into that square must succeed at a DC 14 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check or take 1d4 piercing damage and 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). A creature taking persistent bleed damage from caltrops takes a –5-foot penalty to its Speed. Spending an Interact action to pluck the caltrops free reduces the DC to stop the bleeding. Once a creature takes damage from caltrops, enough caltrops are ruined that other creatures moving into the square are safe.

 Deployed caltrops can be salvaged and reused if no creatures took damage from them. Otherwise, enough caltrops are ruined that they can't be salvaged.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Caltrops

</traits>

This satchel includes 50 feet of rope, pulleys, a dozen pitons, a hammer, a grappling hook, and one set of crampons. Climbing kits allow you to attach yourself to the wall you're [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), moving half as quickly as usual (minimum 5 feet) but letting you attempt a DC 5 flat check whenever you critically fail to prevent a fall. A single kit has only enough materials for one climber; each climber needs their own kit. If you wear your climbing kit, you can access it as part of a Climb action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This small wooden box contains cosmetics, false facial hair, spirit gum, and a few simple wigs. You usually need a disguise kit to set up a disguise in order to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) someone using the [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) skill. If you've crafted a large number of disguises, you can replenish your cosmetics supply with replacement cosmetics suitable for the type of your disguise kit. You can draw and replace a worn disguise kit as part of the action that uses it.

</column>

A formula book holds the formulas necessary to make items other than the common equipment from this chapter; [alchemists](/Classes.aspx?ID=1) typically get one for free. Each formula book can hold the formulas for up to 100 different items. Formulas can also appear on parchment sheets, tablets, and almost any other medium; there's no need for you to copy them into a specific book as long as you can keep them on hand to reference them.

You can throw a grappling hook with a rope tied to it to make a climb easier. To anchor a grappling hook, make an attack roll with the [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) trait against a DC depending on the target, typically at least DC 20. On a success, your hook has a firm hold, but on a critical failure, the hook seems like it will hold but actually falls when you're partway up.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can manacle someone who is willing or otherwise at your mercy as an [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=66) activity taking 10-30 seconds depending on the creature's size and how many manacles you apply. A two-legged creature with its legs bound takes a -15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds, and a two-handed creature with its wrists bound has to succeed at a DC 5 flat check any time it uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action or else that action fails. This DC may be higher depending on how tightly the manacles constrain the hands. A creature bound to a stationary object is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). For creatures with more or fewer limbs, the GM determines what effect manacles have, if any. Freeing a creature from poor manacles requires two successful DC 17 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks, simple manacles requires three successes at DC 22, average manacles require four successes at DC 27, good manacles require five successes at DC 32, and superior manacles require six successes at DC 42.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Handheld instruments include bagpipes, a small set of chimes, small drums, fiddles and viols, flutes and recorders, small harps, lutes, trumpets, and similarly sized instruments. The GM might rule that an especially large handheld instrument (like a tuba) has greater Bulk. Heavy instruments such as large drums, a full set of chimes, and keyboard instruments are less portable and generally need to be stationary while being played. A virtuoso instrument gives a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks using that instrument.

You can use oil to fuel lanterns, but you can also set a pint of oil aflame and throw it. You must first spend an Interact action preparing the oil, then throw it with another action as a ranged attack. If you hit, it splatters on the creature or in a single 5-foot square you target. You must succeed at a DC 10 flat check for the oil to ignite successfully when it hits. If the oil ignites, the target takes 1d6 fire damage.

---

You need thieves' tools to [Pick Locks](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) or [Disable Devices](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) (of some types) using the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) skill. If your thieves' tools are broken, you can repair them by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your tools; this doesn't require using the [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) action. If you wear your thieves' tools, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them. 

You can use a set of tools with 1 hand if you're [wearing them](/Rules.aspx?ID=1456) or 2 if you're holding them.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thieves' Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 291

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You need thieves' tools to [Pick Locks](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) or [Disable Devices](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) (of some types) using the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) skill. If your thieves' tools are broken, you can repair them by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your tools; this doesn't require using the [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) action. If you wear your thieves' tools, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them. 

You can use a set of tools with 1 hand if you're [wearing them](/Rules.aspx?ID=1456) or 2 if you're holding them.

<row gap="medium">

---

This entry is a catchall for basic hand tools that don’t have a specific adventuring purpose. A hoe, shovel, or sledgehammer is a long tool, and a hand drill, ice hook, or trowel is a short tool. A tool can usually be used as an improvised weapon, dealing 1d4 damage for a short tool or 1d6 for a long tool. The GM determines the damage type that’s appropriate or adjusts the damage if needed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scholarly Journal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=64)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 291

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Scholarly journals are uncommon. Each scholarly journal is a folio on a very specific topic, such as vampires or the history of a single town or neighborhood of a city. If you spend 1 minute referencing an academic journal before attempting a skill check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the subject, you gain a +1 item bonus to the check. A compendium of journals costs five times as much as a single journal and requires both hands to use; each compendium contains several journals and grants its bonus on a broader topic, such as all undead or a whole city. The GM determines what scholarly journals are available in any location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scholarly Journal

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Survey Map](/Equipment.aspx?ID=65)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 291

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Maps are uncommon. Most maps you can find are simple and functional. A survey map details a single location in excellent detail. One of these maps gives you a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks and any skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), provided the checks are related to the location detailed on the map. Maps sometimes come in atlases, containing a number of maps of the same quality, often on similar topics. An atlas costs five times as much as a single map and requires both hands to use. The GM determines what maps are available in any location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Survey Map

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

Paid laborers can provide services for you. Unskilled hirelings can perform simple manual labor and are untrained at most skills. Skilled hirelings have expert proficiency in a particular skill. Hirelings are level 0. If a skill check is needed, an untrained hireling has a +0 modifier, while a skilled hireling has a +4 modifier in their area of expertise and +0 for other skill checks. Hirelings’ rates double if they’re going adventuring with you.

Spellcasting services, listed on Table 6–15, are uncommon. Having a spell cast for you requires finding a spellcaster who knows and is willing to cast it. It’s hard to find someone who can cast higher-level spells, and uncommon spells typically cost at least 100% more, if you can find someone who knows them at all. Spells that take a long time to cast (over 1 minute) usually cost 25% more. You must pay any cost listed in the spell in addition to the Price on the table.

Spellcasting services, listed on Table 6–15, are uncommon. Having a spell cast for you requires finding a spellcaster who knows and is willing to cast it. It’s hard to find someone who can cast higher-level spells, and uncommon spells typically cost at least 100% more, if you can find someone who knows them at all. Spells that take a long time to cast (over 1 minute) usually cost 25% more. You must pay any cost listed in the spell in addition to the Price on the table.

---

Spellcasting services, listed on Table 6–15, are uncommon. Having a spell cast for you requires finding a spellcaster who knows and is willing to cast it. It’s hard to find someone who can cast higher-level spells, and uncommon spells typically cost at least 100% more, if you can find someone who knows them at all. Spells that take a long time to cast (over 1 minute) usually cost 25% more. You must pay any cost listed in the spell in addition to the Price on the table.
